sections.paragraph:
ἀνταποδίδωμι fut. ἀνταποδώσω; 2 aor. ἀνταπέδωκα (2 pl.-εδώκατε Gen 44:4; 3 pl.-έδωκαν Mel., P. 72, 531); 2 aor. inf. ἀνταποδοῦναι; 1 fut. pass. ἀνταποδοθήσομαι (s. ἀποδίδωμι; Hdt.+) lit. ‘give back, tender in repayment’.

① to practice reciprocity with respect to an obligation, repay, pay back, requite τινί τι (PLond II, 413, 8 [IV a.d.] ἵνʼ ἀνταποδώσω σοι τὴν ἀγάπην; Sb 7600, 8f ἀ. τὰς χαρίτας; cp. SEG VIII, 549, 33; Pr 25:22; Sir 30:6; 1 Macc 10:27) εὐχαριστίαν τῷ θεῷ ἀ. requite God with thanks 1 Th 3:9; without obj. οὐκ ἔχουσιν ἀ. σοι they have no way to repay you Lk 14:14 (cp. EpJer 33; the thought strikes both a neg. and a positive note for one rooted in Gr-Rom. tradition: on the one hand, the virtuous pers. should be more interested in giving than in taking, but one ought to give to the right kind of pers.; s. e.g. Aristot., EN 1120a). Pass. (Jos., Ant. 14, 212) ἀνταποδοθήσεται αὐτῷ it will be paid back to him Ro 11:35 (Is 40:14 v.l.).

② to exact retribution, repay, pay back τινί τι (PGM 3, 7; 115; Lev 18:25; Ps 7:5; 34:12 al.) ἀ. τοῖς θλίβουσιν ὑμᾶς θλῖψιν 2 Th 1:6. Abs. ἐγὼ ἀνταποδώσω I will repay Ro 12:19; Hb 10:30 (both Dt 32:35).—DELG s.v. δίδωμι. M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

inf. inf. = infinitive

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

obj. obj. = object, objective

neg. neg. = negative

Gr-Rom. Gr-Rom. = Greco-Roman (gener. in contrast to Israelite/Christian tradition)

pers. pers. = person(s)

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 87
